# `rule-catalog/collected/` — machine-generated rule imports

**Everything under this tree is machine-generated by the collector
pipeline.** It is the raw output of

    fdai catalog collect --source <source-id>

for the sources declared in
[../sources/](../sources/). Human authoring belongs in
[../catalog/](../catalog/) (curated, hand-reviewed) - never here.

## Why a separate tree?

The upstream `catalog/` loader is intentionally non-recursive so a
human-authored rule and a machine-imported reference cannot silently
collide in the T0 hot path. The `collected/` tree is:

- **Off the default T0 hot path** - a rule here does not fire until a
  [profile](../profiles/) or a fork explicitly references it by id.
- **Regenerable** - `fdai catalog collect --source <id>` re-produces
  the exact same tree from the pinned source revision, so a diff on
  this directory is a deterministic function of source-repo drift.
- **Reference-only by default** - each entry carries
  `check_logic.kind: expression` with a `reference:` pointing back at
  the source policy definition. Actual Rego evaluation requires an
  upstream curator (or a fork) to author the check logic and
  re-declare the rule under `catalog/`.

## Current contents

| Source id | Origin | Rule count | Layout |
|-----------|--------|-----------:|--------|
| `azure_policy` | Azure/azure-policy `built-in-policies/policyDefinitions/` | ~4700-5100 | `azure-builtin/<AzureCategory>/*.yaml` |

## Consuming these rules

A downstream profile references them by id, exactly like any other
rule. Example:

```yaml
# rule-catalog/profiles-overrides/customer-a.yaml
id: customer-a
title: (Customer A) posture
extends: [strict]
rules:
  - id: azure-builtin.object-storage.secure-transfer-to-storage-accounts-should-be-enabled
    mode: enforce
```

The `ProfileRegistry.resolve()` call flattens the extend chain and
returns the resolved rule set; the composition root wires it into
`RuleIndex` + `T0Engine`.

## Not the fork's responsibility

A fork MAY re-run the collector against a newer pinned commit and
land the resulting diff via PR. Upstream ships this tree so the fork
does NOT need to run any tooling on day one - the reference material
is already present.

## Related

- [../catalog/](../catalog/) - hand-authored T0-ready rules.
- [../profiles/](../profiles/) - named bundles that reference rules
  from either tree.
